note: seq=5 (queue): fast failure detection + INSTANT alerting. Fetch+sha-verify scripts/atlas_healthcheck.py; install atlas-health.service+timer (every ~30s, AccuracySec=1s, SEPARATE from the 2-min deploy pull). Probe checks pg-accepting, autopull timer alive, disk %, worker liveness (config-driven; skipped honestly until workers exist); on ANY failure it pushes status/<node>/alert-<ts>.json to the repo within seconds + logs. Apply-time step runs --selftest (validates the probe is functional; does NOT fail the apply on a currently-unhealthy condition). PLACEHOLDER seq=5 -- renumber to last_seq+1 when you publish.

steps.note: seq=5: install 30s health probe with instant repo alerting

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/health/atlas_healthcheck.py':'7cf4a63a74b65ec17443f0d922c1c705fe2c9ccd5d5c873a6e476260d4f40b22'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS fast health probe (instant repo alerting)
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/health/atlas_healthcheck.py --once
TimeoutStartSec=60

[Unit]
Description=Run ATLAS health probe every 30s

[Timer]
OnBootSec=45s
OnUnitActiveSec=30s
AccuracySec=1s
Unit=atlas-health.service

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
